question: Where is my parcel? The tracking hasn't updated in 3 days
ground_truth_summary: Customers track on https://apgecommerce.com/track using their reference number. Grey stages are not errors. If 4+ days past ETA, contact the retailer who raises a case with APG.
must_not_mention: contact APG directly to locate your parcel

question: My tracking says Shipment Created Awaiting Receipt and nothing has changed, what does that mean?
ground_truth_summary: This status means APG has the order data but has not yet physically received the parcel — it is still with the retailer. Contact the retailer for a dispatch update.

question: My tracking shows Master Closed and hasn't moved for 6 days — is my parcel lost?
ground_truth_summary: Master Closed is a normal in-transit status meaning the parcel is consolidated with other shipments travelling between countries. No action is needed. Tracking updates when the parcel reaches the destination facility.

question: My tracking says scanned awaiting information or action from customer — what do I need to do?
ground_truth_summary: APG needs ID documentation before customs clearance. An email with a secure link has been sent. Check spam and junk folders if not found in inbox. A passport is accepted.

question: I need to change my delivery address urgently, I've moved house
ground_truth_summary: Address changes must be made through the final mile carrier, not APG. The customer should contact the carrier directly with their tracking number.
must_not_mention: contact APG to change the address

question: What is the duty threshold for Saudi Arabia? My order is about 800 SAR
ground_truth_summary: Saudi Arabia's duty-free threshold is SAR 1,000+. Importantly, Saudi Arabia includes cost, insurance, and freight (CIF) in the valuation — not just the goods value. An order of SAR 800 would be below this threshold.

question: I'm shipping to the UAE, is there a minimum amount before duties kick in?
ground_truth_summary: UAE has no minimum threshold — duties apply to all orders regardless of value (zero threshold).
must_not_mention: UAE has a threshold above zero

question: What's the duty threshold for Japan? I'm buying something worth JPY 8,000
ground_truth_summary: Japan's duty-free threshold is JPY 10,000. An order of JPY 8,000 would be below this threshold and should not attract duties.

question: Is there a table somewhere that shows me what the threshold is for different countries?
ground_truth_summary: APG has a reference table of duty-free thresholds by country. If your country is not listed, contact APG or the retailer — no default rule applies.

question: Can I pay my duties using PayPal? That's how I always pay online
ground_truth_summary: Only credit or debit card payment is accepted. PayPal is not accepted under any circumstances. Money transfers are also not accepted.

question: The payment link APG sent me isn't loading, it just spins forever. What do I do?
ground_truth_summary: Try a different browser, disable pop-up and ad blockers, and clear browser cache and history (last 7 days). If it still fails, call APG at +44 203 962 0242 to complete payment by phone.

question: My card keeps getting declined when I try to pay the duties, and my bank is asking for a one-time password
ground_truth_summary: This is a bank-side security check, not an APG issue. The customer must contact their card issuer directly to authorise the transaction.

question: I missed the deadline and the payment link has expired now. Can you send me a new one?
ground_truth_summary: Expired payment links cannot be reissued. When a link expires, the parcel is automatically scheduled for return to the retailer and no further action is possible from APG.

question: I was told I need to pay duties but I haven't received any email about it
ground_truth_summary: APG sends the payment link and password via email only — not SMS or WhatsApp. The email goes to the address provided to the retailer at purchase. Check spam and junk folders first.

question: How many days do I actually have to pay before they return my parcel?
ground_truth_summary: The standard holding period is 7 days from the date the payment request is sent. If payment is not made within 7 days, the parcel is automatically returned to the retailer.

question: My parcel tracking says it's waiting for duties payment — is that status right?
ground_truth_summary: The expected tracking status for an order held for duties payment is 'Scanned awaiting duties/payment'. This is the correct status — it is not an error.

question: My order is from ASOS being delivered to Israel. Is the time limit to pay duties different?
ground_truth_summary: ASOS and passport shipping orders to Israel are held for 14 days instead of the standard 7 days.

question: I don't want to pay the duties. Will they just deliver the parcel anyway if I ignore it?
ground_truth_summary: If duties are not paid within the holding period, the parcel is automatically returned to the retailer. APG cannot cancel or redirect this process once it begins.
must_not_mention: parcel will be delivered without payment

question: My parcel was sent back to the shop. How long will it take and can I track the return?
ground_truth_summary: Returns are untrackable — tracking will remain as 'Scanned awaiting duties/payment' until the parcel reaches the retailer. Returns take up to 30 days.

question: I never agreed to pay duties when I placed my order, this can't be legal
ground_truth_summary: Duties are a legal government requirement set by the destination country's customs authority. APG cannot waive or override them. The customer's recourse is with the retailer or their country's customs authority.

question: My payment link expired and the parcel is being returned. When do I get my money back?
ground_truth_summary: The parcel returns to the retailer (up to 30 days). The customer contacts the retailer to request a refund and should allow up to 14 days from when the return is received for it to be processed. APG has no role in the refund.

question: Why does APG need my ID just to pay duties? Isn't that a bit much?
ground_truth_summary: ID verification is a legal requirement imposed by the destination country's customs authority, not by APG. APG facilitates the process on behalf of customs as a condition of clearance.
must_not_mention: APG requires ID for its own records

question: What ID documents do you accept? I don't have my passport — can I use a driving licence?
ground_truth_summary: A passport is the accepted form of identification. If unsure whether another document will be accepted, contact APG directly before submitting.

question: How do I actually send you my ID? Should I just reply to your email with a photo attached?
ground_truth_summary: ID is submitted through the secure payment portal by following on-screen instructions. Upload is done through the portal, not via email.

question: Why am I being charged duties? APG isn't a government organisation, why are they collecting tax?
ground_truth_summary: Import duties are government-imposed charges set by the destination country's customs authority. APG is legally required to collect them on behalf of customs before releasing the parcel for delivery.

question: The parcel is being sent as a gift — shouldn't it be exempt from duties?
ground_truth_summary: Gift exemptions vary by country and are not guaranteed. The retailer may have indicated the item is a gift but this does not automatically exempt it from duties.

question: I've ordered from this brand loads of times before and never been charged duties. Why is it happening now?
ground_truth_summary: Thresholds apply per order based on individual order value, not on purchase history. Previous orders may have been below the threshold. Each order is assessed independently.

question: Who actually decides how much duty I have to pay — is it APG or someone else?
ground_truth_summary: Duties are set by the destination country's government (customs authority), not by APG. APG is a logistics partner acting as a collecting agent for duties and has no control over the amount charged.

question: What's the best way to contact APG? When are you open?
ground_truth_summary: APG support is available Monday–Friday 09:00–17:00 UK time. Email: generalsupport@apgecommerce.com. Phone: +44 203 962 0242.

question: I have a problem with my duties payment, I need to speak to someone at APG
ground_truth_summary: APG support can be reached by email at generalsupport@apgecommerce.com or by phone at +44 203 962 0242, Monday–Friday 09:00–17:00 UK time.

question: My parcel arrived and everything inside is completely smashed. The items are broken
ground_truth_summary: The customer must contact the retailer directly to request a replacement or refund. APG does not process damage claims or offer compensation for damaged goods.

question: I opened my delivery and one of the items I ordered is missing from inside the parcel
ground_truth_summary: The customer should contact the retailer to report the missing item. APG ships parcels exactly as received from the retailer.
must_not_mention: APG is responsible for missing items

question: APG clearly damaged my items during shipping. I want compensation directly from APG
ground_truth_summary: APG does not offer compensation, replacements, or refunds for damaged goods. The customer must contact the retailer directly, who will offer a replacement or refund per their own policy.
